description.summary: The Yowie is a large, ape-like bipedal creature reported throughout Australia, particularly in eastern regions.
description.full: The Yowie is a large, ape-like bipedal creature reported throughout Australia, particularly in eastern regions. Described as standing 6-12 feet tall with reddish-brown hair covering its body, the Yowie is the Australian counterpart to similar cryptid hominids like Bigfoot and Yeti. Aboriginal traditions of the Yowie predate European contact by thousands of years, with different names across various Aboriginal nations including Doolagahl, Thoolagal, Tjangara, and Yahoo. These ancient traditions are integral to Indigenous knowledge systems and spiritual beliefs. Modern sightings continue in areas including the Gold Coast hinterland, Blue Mountains of New South Wales, and Queensland rainforests, with notable cases like the 1970s Kilcoy Yowie generating significant media attention and numerous highway sightings reported since the 1990s.

physical.morphology: See README.md for detailed physical description
